reasoning: FIDE publishes its ranking lists on the 1st of every month, and Wikipedia updates shortly thereafter. For the Jan 11 resolution, no new FIDE list will be released, meaning Assaubayeva's rank will remain precisely 11, strictly satisfying 'as high or higher' (<=11). For the Feb 3 resolution, the February 1 list will apply. Assaubayeva is participating in Tata Steel Wijk aan Zee (classical); however, the tournament concludes on February 1, which falls past the cutoff date for the February rating list (which captures events ending in January). Consequently, her ratings and those of her main rival Divya Deshmukh (tied closely behind her on tiebreaks) will remain static, almost assuring she holds Rank 11. By April 4, the April 1 rating list will reflect major rating changes from the Tata Steel Challengers and other tournaments (e.g., Prague Chess Festival). Given the intense competition in these events and her razor-thin tiebreaker lead over the 12th place, the variance over this longer horizon pushes the probability close to a coin flip.
